strengths: Strong alignment with primary stack: active TypeScript, Node.js, and React experience in recent roles, plus current AI-agent and MCP tooling work at re:cinq directly matching the AI agent development requirement

Deep, demonstrated testing culture: TDD practitioner across multiple jobs, authored open-source testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and ran TDD/testing workshops — well beyond checkbox familiarity
Broad infrastructure competence: AWS CDK at Optilyz, Kubernetes and Docker for GISCollective, MongoDB in production at scale, and Git-native workflow with CI/CD history across multiple roles
concerns: Nest.js is entirely absent from the resume — the primary backend framework for this role — leaving uncertainty about framework-specific patterns (modules, guards, interceptors, DI)
Kotlin is listed as a secondary requirement and is not mentioned anywhere; Java appears only in passing for robotic-arm work, not backend services
No explicit mention of Vitest or Playwright; testing experience is deep but concentrated in D-language frameworks and older Jest-era tooling, requiring validation of current testing-stack fluency
response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned fullstack engineer whose recent work at re:cinq maps almost directly onto the role: TypeScript, Node.js, React, AI-agent infrastructure, and developer tooling. His 20-year arc shows consistent growth from junior freelancer to staff-level contributor across diverse domains, and his testing philosophy (TDD, open-source frameworks, workshops) is unusually strong. The main gap is Nest.js — the listed primary backend framework — but given his Node.js depth and architectural range, onboarding to Nest.js patterns is a low-risk ramp. Kotlin is a minor miss given it is secondary. Overall this is a high-fit candidate whose current work is essentially a preview of this job.
